rows.query: status of under construction hydro projects of NHPC

draft.notice: Generated from NHPC's past replies and the selected supporting documents only. Verify every figure and claim against the cited sources before use. This is not an approved reply.
draft.subject: Information for framing the reply of Parliament question regarding status of under construction hydro projects of NHPC
draft.opening: The information regarding the status of under construction hydro projects of NHPC is as under:-

parts.text: NHPC (including Joint Ventures) is implementing 09 Projects with an aggregate installed capacity of 5999 MW which are under construction (2021-nov-dec lok_sabha 8530). Out of 10 under construction projects mentioned in the list, NHPC (including Joint Venture) is implementing 02 Projects (2026-jan-mar rajya_sabha 250).

The 2000 MW Subansiri Lower HE Project in Arunachal Pradesh / Assam on Subansiri River is in the construction stage (2023-nov-jan rajya_sabha s-1443). Its physical progress is 97.84% as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The project was likely to be commissioned in May 2025 (2023-nov-jan rajya_sabha s-1443). However, the present expected date of completion is March 2027, with Unit #1 by 20.03.2026, Unit #2 by 23.12.2025, Unit #3 by 01.02.2026, Unit #4 by 08.05.2026, Unit #5 by 15.07.2026, Unit #6 by 15.09.2026, Unit #7 by 28.02.2027, and Unit #8 by 31.03.2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The time overrun is 198 months (approved by CEA: 170.5 Months) (DOC:projects_progress/9). The sanctioned cost was Rs 6285.33 crore (Dec'2002 PL) and the anticipated cost is Rs 30071.97 crore (based on project completion by Dec-2026), resulting in a cost overrun of Rs 23787 crore (378%) (DOC:projects_progress/9).

The 850 MW Ratle HE Project is being implemented through RHPCL, a joint venture between NHPC and JKSPDC (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 1743). Presently, 31.29% physical progress has been achieved as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The project was scheduled for commissioning in Nov 2028 (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 1743), but the present expected date of completion is November 2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The time overrun is 33 months (DOC:projects_progress/9).

The 1000 MW Pakal Dul HE Project is being implemented through CVPPL, a joint venture between NHPC and JKSPDC (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 1743). Presently, 83.00% physical progress has been achieved as on 30.06.2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The project was scheduled for commissioning in Dec 2026 (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 1743), but the present expected date of completion is March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9). The time overrun is 83 months (DOC:projects_progress/9). The sanctioned cost was Rs 8112.12 crore (Mar'13 PL) and the anticipated cost is Rs 12728 crore (at completion cost), resulting in a cost overrun of Rs 4616 crore (57%) (DOC:projects_progress/9).

Other under construction projects include Dibang MPP (2880 MW) with 17.78% physical progress as on 30.06.2026 and expected completion in Feb 2032; Teesta-VI (500 MW) with 73.48% physical progress as on 30.06.2026 and expected completion in Sep 2029; Rangit IV (120 MW) with 97.34% physical progress as on 30.06.2026; Kiru (624 MW) with 87.00% physical progress as on 30.06.2026 and expected completion in Mar 2027; Kwar (540 MW) with 36.52% physical progress as on 30.06.2026 and expected completion in Mar 2028; Uri-I, Stage-II (240 MW) and Dulhasti, Stage-II (260 MW) with expected completion in Nov 2029 and Nov 2028/Nov 2029 respectively (DOC:projects_progress/9).

key_points.point: NHPC (including Joint Ventures) is implementing 09 Projects with an aggregate installed capacity of 5999 MW under construction.
Subansiri Lower HE Project (2000 MW) is in construction stage, with 97.84% physical progress as on 30.06.2026 and expected completion in March 2027.
Ratle HE Project (850 MW) has 31.29% physical progress as on 30.06.2026 and expected completion in November 2026.
Pakal Dul HE Project (1000 MW) has 83.00% physical progress as on 30.06.2026 and expected completion in March 2027.
Dibang MPP (2880 MW) has 17.78% physical progress as on 30.06.2026 and expected completion in Feb 2032.
Teesta-VI (500 MW) has 73.48% physical progress as on 30.06.2026 and expected completion in Sep 2029.
Rangit IV (120 MW) has 97.34% physical progress as on 30.06.2026.
Kiru (624 MW) has 87.00% physical progress as on 30.06.2026 and expected completion in Mar 2027.
Kwar (540 MW) has 36.52% physical progress as on 30.06.2026 and expected completion in Mar 2028.
Uri-I, Stage-II (240 MW) and Dulhasti, Stage-II (260 MW) are expected to be completed in Nov 2029 and Nov 2028/Nov 2029 respectively.

contradictions.past: The Project is scheduled to be fully commissioned by May 2026. (2024-nov rajya_sabha u-617)
contradictions.current: The present expected date of completion for the Subansiri Lower (8x250 = 2000 MW) Project is March 2027, with individual units scheduled for commissioning between December 2025 and March 2027 (U#1: 20.03.26 | U#2: 23.12.25 | U#3: 01.02.26 | U#4: 08.05.26 | U#5: 15 Jul-26 | U#6: 15 Sep-26 | U#7 : 28 Feb-27 | U#8 : 31 Mar-27) as on 30.06.2026.

sources.question_text: (c) By when the Subansiri Lower Hydroelectric Project will be completed ; and
sources.answer_text: The Project is scheduled to be fully commissioned by May 2026. However it is pertinent to mention here that an Expert Committee has been constituted by Ministry of Power vide order dated 23.11.2023 comprising of Experts from CEA, CWC, GSI, IIT Roorkee, NIRM, IIRS Dehradun & Wadia Institute of Himalayan Geology for assessing the ground situation and to recommend long term stability measures for the left bank slope. The committee has visited the site from 25.11.2023 to 27.11.2023 and subsequent meetings held on 02.04.2024, 10.04.2024, 18.04.24, 30.04.2024, 23.08.2024 & 17.10.2024. Further, the committee has visited the site from 20.11.2024 to 22.11.2024. The report of Expert committee is awaited. The recommendations of the committee will be implemented during this working season. Accordingly, the 3 units of 250 MW each (i.e. capacity of 750 MW) will be commissioned by May 2025 and balance 5 units (i.e. capacity of 1250 MW) shall be commissioned by May 2026.

sources.question_text: (c) By when the Subansiri Lower Hydroelectric Project will be completed ; and

sources.question_text: (a) What is the status of the Subansiri Hydro-Project and when is it likely to be commissioned?
sources.answer_text: The 2000 MW Subansiri Lower HE Poject is in advanced stage of execution and commissioning. rows.prompt: use the progress report for physical progress figures

draft.subject: Information for framing the reply of Parliament question regarding progress of hydro projects in Jammu and Kashmir
draft.opening: May be replied by Ministry of Power. As far as NHPC is concerned, the information regarding progress of hydro projects in Jammu and Kashmir is as under:-

parts.text: NHPC is constructing four Hydroelectric Projects with an aggregate installed capacity of 3014 MW in the Union Territory of Jammu & Kashmir through joint ventures (CVPPL and RHPCL) (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 265, 2024-jul rajya_sabha s-2577). The timeline of these projects has been revised due to factors such as the COVID-19 pandemic, geological issues, and local issues (2025-monsoon lok_sabha 9585).

The projects under construction include Ratle (850 MW), Pakal Dul (1000 MW), Kiru (624 MW), and Kwar (540 MW) (2025-monsoon lok_sabha 9585, 2024-jul rajya_sabha s-2577). As on 30.06.2026, the physical progress for these projects is: Ratle (97.34%), Pakal Dul (31.29%), Kiru (83.00%), and Kwar (87.00%) (DOC:projects_progress/9).

The present expected dates of completion for these projects are: Ratle by November 2026, Pakal Dul by March 2027, Kiru by March 2027, and Kwar by March 2028 (DOC:projects_progress/9). These projects have experienced time overruns due to various reasons including delay in award of major works packages, poor geological conditions, impact of COVID-19, local issues, and labour strikes (DOC:projects_progress/9).

In addition to the projects under construction, four Hydroelectric Projects aggregating to 3176 MW, including the 1856 MW Sawalkot project, are under various stages of statutory clearances (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 265, 2025-monsoon lok_sabha 9585). Further, 3 Hydroelectric Projects are under clearances and one Hydro project is under Survey & Investigation stage (2024-jul rajya_sabha s-2577). The projects are being reviewed for their timely completion, with regular review meetings conducted by the Ministry of Power, Central Electricity Authority, inter-ministerial committee of IWT and Home Ministry, and monitoring by the PMO (2025-nov-dec rajya_sabha 265).

sources.question_text: (b) if so, the reasons therefor and the details of the under construction power projects in various parts of the country, State/UT-wise including Odisha:
sources.answer_text: May be replied by Ministry of Power / CEA. As far as NHPC Ltd. is concerned, it is constructing 15 Power Projects with aggregate installed capacity of 10449 MW. The detail of same is as under: Amount in ₹ crore Out of these construction Projects, eight projects are running behind their prescribed schedule. The reasons of time overrun of these Projects are as given below: *26 MW out of total 65 MW Synchronized and Commissioned on 09.07.2022. 1. PARBATI-II HE PROJECT The original CCEA cost of Parbati-II HE Project was ₹ 3,919.59 crore at Dec'2001 PL. The Project has time over run of 175 months. Now, the anticipated cost of the Project is ₹ 11134.54 crore (July' 22 PL). REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Initial delay due to delay in statutory clearance.  Geological surprises.  Week geological conditions in HRT.  Ban on use of aggregate from August 2007 to March 2008 by Hon'ble High court, Shimla.  Poor performance of contractors and re-award of balance works of HRT and Nallah works.  Nationwide lock down due to covid-19.  Natural calamities. 2. SUBANSIRI LOWER HE PROJECT The original CCEA approved Cost of Subansiri Lower HE Project was ₹6,285.33 crore at Dec'2002 PL. The Project has overall time over run of 165 months. Presently, the anticipated cost of the Project is ₹ 21,247.54 crore (completion cost). REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Delay in transfer of Forest Land.  Law & Order problems (Intermittent Forcible Stoppages / Bandh by locals from Dec 2011)  Ban on construction works by Hon'ble National Green Tribunal (NGT) Kolkata vide order dated 11.12.2015. The construction work resumed on 15.10.2019 after clearance from Hon'ble NGT.  Nation wide lock down due to covid-19.  Natural calamities  Time for executing additional works suggested by Dam Design Review Panel (DDRP). 3. PAKALDUL HE PROJECT Pakaldul HE Project has time over run of 78 months. REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Cancellation of tenders due to less response and higher cost quoted by the bidders.  Slope failure at HRT adit portal.  Frequent intermittent strikes.  Nation wide lock down due to covid-19. 4. KIRU HE PROJECT Kiru HE Project has time over run of 30 months. REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Delay in award of works due to issue related to Subordinate debt for the Project.  Nation wide lock down due to covid-19.  Delay in supply & commissioning of tower crane for dam concreting on account of delayed supply of various parts from different countries. 5. TEESTA VI HE PROJECT Teesta-VI HE Project has time over run of 29 months. REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Delay in award of Major works package.  Week geological conditions in HRT.  Nation wide lock down due to covid-19. 6. RANGIT IV HE PROJECT Rangit-IV HE Project has time over run of 3 months. REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Delay in award of Major works package for want of Security clearance from MHA, GoI in respect of foreign company participating in the tender.  Nation wide lock down due to covid-19. 7. RATLE IV HE PROJECT Ratle HE Project has time over run of 3 months. REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Contractual delay. 8. KALPI SOLAR PROJECT Kalpi solar Project has time over run of 27 months. 26 MW capacity out of 65 MW was commissioned in July 2022. REASONS FOR TIME OVER RUN  Supply chain disruption in PV Module. Imposition of Basic Custom Duty w.e.f. 01.04.2022 resulting into limited availability and sharp rise in the prices of solar modules  Nation wide lock down due to covid-19.

parts.text:
एनएचपीसी लिमिटेड की निर्माणाधीन परियोजनाओं की वर्तमान स्थिति निम्नानुसार है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार):-

1. **सुबनसिरी लोअर (2000 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 2000 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 97.84% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 424.29% तथा संशोधित/अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 88.68% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। इस परियोजना के लिए संचयी व्यय 30.06.2026 तक 26,668 करोड़ रुपये है। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि मार्च 2027 है, जिसमें यूनिट 1: 20.03.2026, यूनिट 2: 23.12.2025, यूनिट 3: 01.02.2026, यूनिट 4: 08.05.2026, यूनिट 5: 15.07.2026, यूनिट 6: 15.09.2026, यूनिट 7: 28.02.2027 और यूनिट 8: 31.03.2027 तक अपेक्षित है (DOC:projects_progress/9)। परियोजना में 198 महीने का समय अधिरोहण है, जिसके मुख्य कारण वन भूमि का हस्तांतरण, पावरहाउस में बैक हिल ढलान की विफलता, स्थानीय आंदोलन, COVID-19 का प्रभाव, अतिरिक्त डाइक कार्य और विविध बाधाएं हैं (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

2. **दिबांग बहुउद्देशीय परियोजना (2880 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 2880 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 17.78% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 15.39% तथा संशोधित/अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 15.39% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। इस परियोजना के लिए संचयी व्यय 30.06.2026 तक 4,904 करोड़ रुपये है। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि फरवरी 2032 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

3. **तीस्ता-VI (500 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 500 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 73.48% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 94.88% तथा संशोधित/अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 64.55% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। इस परियोजना के लिए संचयी व्यय 30.06.2026 तक 5,454 करोड़ रुपये है। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि सितंबर 2029 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

4. **रंगित IV (120 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 120 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 199.27% तथा संशोधित/अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 99.00% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। इस परियोजना के लिए संचयी व्यय 30.06.2026 तक 1,870 करोड़ रुपये है। परियोजना में 30 महीने का समय अधिरोहण है, जिसके मुख्य कारण प्रमुख कार्य पैकेज के अवार्ड में देरी, खराब भूवैज्ञानिक स्थितियां और COVID-19 का प्रभाव हैं (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

5. **रैटल (850 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 850 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 31.29% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 30.97% तथा संशोधित/अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 30.97% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। इस परियोजना के लिए संचयी व्यय 30.06.2026 तक 1,636 करोड़ रुपये है। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि नवंबर 2026 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

6. **पाकल दुल (1000 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 1000 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 83.00% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 118.82% तथा संशोधित/अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 75.73% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। इस परियोजना के लिए संचयी व्यय 30.06.2026 तक 9,639 करोड़ रुपये है। परियोजना में 83 महीने का समय अधिरोहण है, जिसके मुख्य कारण प्रमुख अनुबंध पैकेजों के टेंडरिंग और अवार्ड में देरी, COVID-19 का प्रभाव, टीबीएम एडिट साइट पर बड़ी ढलान विफलता और श्रम हड़ताल/स्थानीय बाधाएं हैं (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

7. **किरू (624 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 624 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 87.00% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 89.97% तथा संशोधित/अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 71.32% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। इस परियोजना के लिए संचयी व्यय 30.06.2026 तक 3,857 करोड़ रुपये है। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि मार्च 2027 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

8. **क्वार (540 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 540 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की भौतिक प्रगति 36.52% है और वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 40.67% तथा संशोधित/अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 40.67% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। इस परियोजना के लिए संचयी व्यय 30.06.2026 तक 1,841 करोड़ रुपये है। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि मार्च 2028 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

9. **उरी-I, स्टेज-II (240 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 240 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 0.92% तथा संशोधित/अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 0.92% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। इस परियोजना के लिए संचयी व्यय 30.06.2026 तक 25 करोड़ रुपये है। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि नवंबर 2029 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

10. **दुलहस्ती, स्टेज-II (260 मेगावाट):** इस परियोजना की स्वीकृत क्षमता 260 मेगावाट है। परियोजना की वित्तीय प्रगति स्वीकृत लागत के अनुसार 2.99% तथा संशोधित/अनुमानित लागत के अनुसार 2.99% है (30.06.2026 की स्थिति के अनुसार)। इस परियोजना के लिए संचयी व्यय 30.06.2026 तक 89 करोड़ रुपये है। परियोजना के पूरा होने की वर्तमान अपेक्षित तिथि नवंबर 2029 है (DOC:projects_progress/9)।

draft.opening: The information in respect of NHPC Ltd. is as under:-

parts.label: (a)
parts.text: As on 30.06.2026, NHPC has 10 hydro projects under construction, including Joint Ventures (JVs). These projects are Subansiri Lower, Dibang MPP, Teesta-VI, Rangit IV, Ratle, Pakal Dul, Kiru, Kwar, Uri-I, Stage-II, and Dulhasti, Stage-II.

parts.label: (b)
parts.text:
The expected commissioning schedule for each project as on 30.06.2026 is as follows:
- Subansiri Lower: March 2027 (Unit #1: 20.03.2026, Unit #2: 23.12.2025, Unit #3: 01.02.2026, Unit #4: 08.05.2026, Unit #5: 15.07.2026, Unit #6: 15.09.2026, Unit #7: 28.02.2027, Unit #8: 31.03.2027) (DOC:projects_progress/9). NHPC's reply of 2025-12-11 stated the expected commissioning date for Subansiri Lower (2000 MW) as March 2027 (2025-nov-dec lok_sabha 5695).
- Dibang MPP: February 2032 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Teesta-VI: September 2029 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Rangit IV: The present expected date of completion is not specified in the provided document (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Ratle: November 2026 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Pakal Dul: March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Kiru: The present expected date of completion is not specified in the provided document (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Kwar: March 2027 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Uri-I, Stage-II: March 2028 (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Dulhasti, Stage-II: November 2029 (DOC:projects_progress/9).

parts.label: (c)
parts.text:
The total investment involved (Sanctioned Cost) for each project as on 30.06.2026 is as follows:
- Subansiri Lower: Rs. 6285.33 crore (Dec'2002 PL) (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Dibang MPP: Rs. 31876.39 crore (May'21 PL) (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Teesta-VI: Rs. 5748.04 crore (July, 2018 PL) (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Rangit IV: Rs. 938.29 crore (Oct'19 PL) (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Ratle: The sanctioned cost is not specified in the provided document (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Pakal Dul: Rs. 5281.94 crore (Nov'18 PL) (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Kiru: Rs. 8112.12 crore (Mar'13 PL) (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Kwar: Rs. 4287.59 crore (Jul'18 PL) (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Uri-I, Stage-II: Rs. 4526.12 crore (Sep'20 PL) (DOC:projects_progress/9).
- Dulhasti, Stage-II: Rs. 2708.95 crore (at Completion) (DOC:projects_progress/9).

NHPC has 09 projects with an aggregate installed capacity of 5999 MW under construction (including Joint ventures) as of December 2021 (2021-nov-dec lok_sabha 8530).

6. Pakal Dul: 1000 MW (4x250 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9)
7. Kiru: 624 MW (4x156 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9)

9. Uri-I, Stage-II: 240 MW (2x120 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9)
10. Dulhasti, Stage-II: 260 MW (2x130 MW) (DOC:projects_progress/9)

sources.question_text: (b) The list of new projects being undertaken by NHPC?
sources.answer_text: Presently, 24 Power Stations with aggregate installed capacity of 7071.20 MW are under operation and 09 Projects with aggregate installed capacity of 5999 MW are under construction (including Joint ventures). Regarding new Projects being undertaken by NHPC, 18 Projects with an aggregate installed capacity of 12887.1 MW (including JV projects) are under clearance and 3 Hydroelectric Projects with an aggregate installed capacity of 1130 MW are under Survey and Investigation. List of these Projects is enclosed at Annexure-I.
